title: Write a Book
subtitle: A small starting shape for drafting a book without losing chapter focus, momentum, and the tool layer.
scope: One manuscript or book project moving from outline into drafted chapters.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholder chapter notes, source material, and tool paths with real manuscript assets.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when you need a first living doc for a book and want to stay focused on chapter progress instead of over-modeling the manuscript.

objective: Make the manuscript legible enough that someone can see what the book is, which chapters are moving, and how the work is being supported.
successCondition: A reader can identify the current chapter set, the state of each major drafting unit, and the tools used to keep the manuscript moving.

callouts.title: How to use this starter
items: Start with the chapter units that matter right now instead of trying to map every theme and storyline up front.
Add narrative mapping only when the manuscript is large enough that continuity and thematic pressure need separate structure.

callout.title: Starter intent
items: Keep the top of the document about the manuscript's current shape, not about every future structural concern.
The section below should make the active drafting units obvious.

data.name: Chapter 1: Return Home
data.synopsis: Lena returns to the island and discovers that the family house has been partially emptied before her arrival.
notes: Use the chapter flow to track the units you are actively trying to finish, not every speculative scene.
This chapter establishes the atmosphere, the missing archive, and the family tension.
dependencies: Needs the precise archive-room memory beat before revision.
May absorb one image from the interview transcript.

data.name: Chapter 3: Archive Room
data.synopsis: Lena and Tomas enter the storage room and realize the missing boxes were removed deliberately, not lost to decay.
notes: This is the first chapter where the book's memory-versus-evidence tension becomes explicit.
dependencies: Depends on the physical layout notes from the field notebook.

data.name: Chapter 5: Kitchen Table
data.synopsis: An evening conversation with Lena's mother forces the hidden debt line into the open.
notes: Keep one not-started chapter visible so the next meaningful drafting move is always explicit.
dependencies: Needs a stable emotional tone for the mother before drafting.

data.name: Universal renderer
data.purpose: Render the canonical manuscript JSON into a shareable HTML snapshot without losing the document structure.
data.whenToUse: Use when you want to review the manuscript state away from the raw JSON or share it with collaborators.
caveats: The HTML snapshot is portable, but the JSON remains the canonical version to update.

data.name: Chapter outline board
data.purpose: Keep a working chapter list and the current shape of the manuscript visible during drafting.
data.whenToUse: Use while deciding what to draft next or when you need a short prose reminder of chapter purpose.
caveats: A flat outline can hide deeper thematic or continuity drift once the manuscript grows.
